title: 775 million people are exposed to floods globally
subtitle: Area and share of population exposed to floods with inundation depth over 50cm[footnote:  An area is exposed to floods if there is more than a 1 percent annual probability of  experiencing flooding with inundation depth of more than  50 centimeters.]
source: Deltares, Fathom, Hill et al.
visdescription: Share of population exposed to floods and location of areas exposed to flood risk

scenes.text: [emphasis: Floods] can severely damage infrastructure and homes, disrupt the economy, and destroy crops and livestock. Impacts become particularly severe when inundation depth exceeds 50 centimeters.[reference: Rentschler] The areas in [emphasis: blue] indicate exposure above this level.

The [emphasis: population exposed to ][emphasis: floods] live near rivers, in areas with low elevation, and near the coast. In The Netherlands, more than one-third of the population is exposed to [emphasis: floods] due to the country’s many rivers and low elevation.

Globally, [emphasis: 775 million people are exposed to] [emphasis: floods.] Half of them live in three populous countries: China, India, and Bangladesh. In Bangladesh, one-third of the population is exposed to [emphasis: floods].
